notes: in my case the game has no controls in fullscreen mode but running it in windowed mode fixed that. i also couldn't get my PS4 controller to work in either windowed or fullscreen mode though more testing needs to be done
title: Praey for the Gods

notes: Performance is about 30 to 50 fps, not very stable. Eventually the mouse stops working in menus as for example the inventory or the main menu, rendering saving or inventory usage impossible. Currently tweaking xboxdrv as this problems does not seem to occure with a gamepad.
title: Praey for the Gods

notes: Game Runs the same as on windows for me framerate is between 40 and 60 fps, but xinput devices can't be configured in full screen and mouse stops working in menues.
title: Praey for the Gods

notes:
I updated my version of dxvk to 0.96 and now the menus work as long as i use mouse and keyboard to navigate them. If i use my steam controller as a mouse the mouse pointer vanishes, but will be there again if i reopen them with mouse and keyboard.
Xinput devices still refuse to work.
title: Praey for the Gods

notes: Ran the first couple of tries, but took a RIDICULOUS amount of time to launch, even on SSD. Like other users have said, controllers don't seem to work, fullscreen or not. FPS hovers between 20-30 at 1080p on max graphics settings. Game crashes upon entering or leaving fullscreen, or alt-tabbing. Third time or so trying to run the game, it wouldn't launch at all. Neither can I verify the game cache or uninstall. Probably an easy fix, but I think I might just wait to try again if/when the game gets a native port.
title: Praey for the Gods

notes: Takes time to launch, but then everything runs perfectly. FPS between 48 and 60. Controller works. No issue detected whatsoever.
title: Praey for the Gods

notes: Runs perfectly and generally is comfortably playable. Rare crashes. Xbox controller works.
title: Praey for the Gods

gpu: NVIDIA GeForce GTX 1060 with Max-Q Design
notes:
Had to add 
WINEDLLOVERRIDES="xaudio2_7=n,b" PULSE_LATENCY_MSEC=120
to the launch options so the sound stuttering would stop. Sometime stuff might fall through the terrain and the performance isn't great, but I am not sure if these issues are Proton specific or just with the game itself.
title: Praey for the Gods

notes: Started and runs just fine without any tweaks, including controller support.
title: Praey for the Gods
